metadata.title: Frontier — Editor's Picks
metadata.description: Hand-picked landmark papers behind the concepts this quiz covers. Curated and reviewed, unlike the live arXiv feed.

entries.title: Attention Is All You Need
entries.why: Introduced the Transformer — an encoder/decoder built entirely on self-attention, no recurrence — that underlies virtually every modern LLM.

entries.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
entries.why: Showed that scaling a decoder-only transformer to 175B parameters unlocks strong few-shot, in-context learning without task-specific fine-tuning.

entries.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
entries.why: Paired a pretrained seq2seq model with a learned dense retriever over a vector index — the blueprint modern RAG pipelines still follow.

entries.title: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
entries.why: Introduced the RLHF fine-tuning recipe used to make base models follow instructions and prefer helpful, honest responses — the technique behind ChatGPT.

entries.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
entries.why: Showed that prompting a model to produce intermediate reasoning steps, rather than jumping to an answer, sharply improves multi-step problem solving.

entries.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
entries.why: Interleaves reasoning traces with tool-calling actions and observations — the loop most modern agent frameworks are built on.

entries.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
entries.why: Replaces much of human feedback with AI-generated critiques judged against a written constitution, reducing reliance on human labelers for safety training.

entries.title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
entries.why: Samples several diverse chain-of-thought paths for the same question and takes the majority final answer, trading extra compute for higher accuracy.
